title: Day 14 — Machine Coding Round 2
summary: 120-minute Week 2 review build under interview conditions — product listing, search, filter, pagination, and shopping cart without referring to previous code.

blocks.content: Day 07 tested Week 1 under pressure. Day 14 tests Week 2. You have 120 minutes to build a mini e-commerce app from scratch — product listing, search, filter, pagination, and cart. No AI. No Google. Only official docs. This mirrors real machine coding rounds at product companies.

blocks.title: Exam conditions
blocks.content: Start a timer. Close AI tools and search engines. Allowed: react.dev, reactrouter.com, typescriptlang.org, developer.mozilla.org. Do NOT open previous day project folders — rebuild muscle memory.

rows: Product API fetch and UI states

items.text: 0–10 min: Scaffold — routes, types, folder structure, CartProvider
10–40 min: Product fetch, grid, filters, search
40–70 min: Pagination + cart reducer with add/remove/quantity
70–100 min: Wire navbar badge, cart page, empty states
100–120 min: Fix build errors, smoke test, README self-review, git push

blocks.title: Prioritization rule
blocks.content: Working cart with add-only beats perfect filters with no cart. Interviewers score end-to-end flow. Ship core paths first, polish last.

src/features/products/ — listing, filters, search. src/features/cart/ — context, reducer, cart page. src/types/ — Product, CartItem. src/hooks/ — useDebounce. App wrapped with CartProvider and BrowserRouter.

blocks.text: Interview Focus — Be Ready to Explain

blocks.content: After the build, review these topics verbally. Interviewers ask WHY not just WHAT.

items.text: How fetch handles loading, error, and retry
Why debounce search — event loop connection
What your custom hook extracts and why
Why Context + reducer for cart vs prop drilling
Route structure if you added detail pages

blocks.title: Event loop question
blocks.content: Expect: 'What runs first — Promise.then or setTimeout?' Answer: microtasks before macrotasks. Connect to debounce timer and fetch .then ordering.

blocks.title: Common failure modes
blocks.content: CartProvider inside one route. Fetch without debounce causing request storm. Mutating cart array in reducer. Missing keys on product list. No loading state — app looks broken during fetch.
Create from scratch: product listing + search + filter + pagination + shopping cart. Log your start and end time. Write self-review in README when done.

items.text: No AI / no Google during build

blocks.title: Week 2 complete
blocks.content: Tomorrow Week 3 begins with Theme Management — advanced patterns, Redux Toolkit, RTK Query, authentication, and performance optimization. Rest tonight. You earned it.
